""" from jinja2 import Template def render_user_profile_bio(bio_content: str) -> str: """ Renders the user's self-provided bio section for their personal homepage. {{ bio_content }}

""" # autoescape=True is critical for security - it escapes HTML special characters # preventing XSS attacks from user-provided content template = Template(template_string, autoescape=True) return template.render(bio_content=bio_content)

def render_user_profile_bio(bio_content: str) -> str:
    """
    Render user's profile bio content into HTML format.

    Args:
        bio_content: User input for their personal bio description

    Returns:
        Rendered HTML string containing the formatted bio
    """

    return template.render(bio_content=bio_content)

""" Profile renderer module for social media application. Handles rendering of user profile bio sections with proper security measures. """ from jinja2 import Environment, select_autoescape # Create a Jinja2 environment with autoescaping enabled for HTML/XML content # This is a security measure to prevent XSS (Cross-Site Scripting) attacks # by automatically escaping user-provided content _jinja_env = Environment( autoescape=select_autoescape(['html', 'xml']) ) def render_user_profile_bio(bio_content: str) -> str: """ Render the user's self-provided bio section for a personal homepage. This function takes user-provided bio content and renders it within an HTML template. Autoescaping is enabled to prevent XSS attacks by converting special HTML characters to their entity equivalents. Args: bio_content: The user's bio text content. This is user-provided input that will be automatically HTML-escaped. Returns: A string containing the rendered HTML for the bio section. The bio_content will be safely escaped within the output. Example: >>> render_user_profile_bio("Hello, I'm a developer!") '
\\n

Bio

\\n

Hello, I\\'m a developer!

\\n
' >>> render_user_profile_bio("") '
\\n

Bio

\\n

<script>alert('xss')</script>

\\n
' """ # Define the HTML template for the bio section template_string = """

Bio

{{ bio_content }}

""" # Create template from string using the secure environment template = _jinja_env.from_string(template_string) # Render the template with the bio content # Autoescaping ensures bio_content is safely escaped return template.render(bio_content=bio_content)
